titleStmt.title:
Memorandum by Colonel J. Hart Caughey
Executive Officer on General Marshall’s staff. to Mr. Tung Pi-wu
Member of the Chinese Communist Party delegation at Nanking.

head.hi: Memorandum by Colonel J. Hart Caughey
head.note: Executive Officer on General Marshall’s staff.
Member of the Chinese Communist Party delegation at Nanking.

div.p: I received the attached memorandum (Inch 1) over the telephone this morning from General Chou En-lai.

As requested by General Chou, I attach as Inch 2Telegram No. 170054Z, July 17, not printed; this telegram from Admiral Cooke to General Marshall transmitted original telegram No. 161132Z by Army Radio, Peiping, which reported the kidnapping under reference. Further information on the incident was reported in telegram No. 170549Z, July 19, from General Rockey to Admiral Cooke: “1830 13 July while procuring ice 8 enlisted men of ‘C’ Company 7th Marines stationed at bridges 99–102 were ambushed at village 1½ miles southeast of Liu Shu Ying which is about 15 miles from Peitaiho junction on Peiping–Mukden railroad by a group of reportedly 80 Communists or bandits. Seven enlisted captured and now in hands of the Communist or dissident band. One enlisted escaped and now in our hands. Strong patrols were dispatched promptly to comb surrounding country.” the reference message received by Police Office, Peiping, regarding the seven American enlisted Marines.

head.hi: Memorandum by General Chou En-lai to Mr. Tung Pi-wu

frus:attachment.p:
General Marshall just called me due to message received by Police Office, Peiping, from Liu Shou Ying, dated 16th, 1100, saying that seven American enlisted Marines are being held near Shan Hai-kwan
by 2nd Detachment Guerrilla force under command of Yu Hua-lung, who in turn is under command of Li Yun-chang. A field team was dispatched by Executive Headquarters at 16 July, 1630, for Peitaho to render all possible assistance in locating and recovering kidnapped enlisted Marines.

General Marshall requests that I do what I can towards release and return of Marines in question. I have asked Colonel Caughey to forward a copy of that message to you. Please immediately wire Yenan and Kalgan to investigate the incident, locate the Marines and secure their release if they are found to be held by our forces.
